sanskrit:
अधियज्ञः कथं को ऽत्र देहे ऽस्मिन् मधुसूदन।
प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयो ऽसि नियतात्मभिः॥
transliteration: adhiyajñaḥ kathaṃ ko ’tra dehe ’smin madhusūdana | prayāṇakāle ca kathaṃ jñeyo ’si niyatātmabhiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Lord-of-sacrifice / how / who / here / in-the-body / in-this / O Madhusūdana / at-death-time / and / how / to-be-known / You-are / by-the-self-controlled
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks how the divine presence that sanctifies every action dwells within the body, and how a person striving for inner stability can remain aware of it during the ultimate transition of death.
psychological_state: Pre-traumatic anticipation: the mind rehearses the feared moment of losing continuity of identity and wonders whether its chosen sacred focus will survive the stress of dying.
trigger_scenario: Lying in the MRI machine awaiting biopsy results, suddenly realizing you may not remember your mantra or your loved ones if the sedation or the final fog hits.
anchor_text: Remember the eternal within as life ends.
